channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump blames Biden, Harris for Iran's attack on Israel: 'Very close to global catastrophe'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump slammed President Biden and Vice President Kamala Harris after Iran launched an unprecedented ballistic missiles attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans eager to keep House control are open to Trump’s SALT reversal
item.description: Republicans are open to raising the state and local tax (SALT) deduction cap that the party championed and put in place under former President Trump seven years ago as they aim to win total control of government in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Live updates: Vance, Walz spar over abortion, immigration and guns
item.description: Ohio Sen. JD Vance (R) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) are meeting on the debate stage, in what could be the last major televised event in the race for the White House before November.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: VP candidates Vance and Walz meet in last scheduled debate for 2024 tickets
item.description: Barring a late addition to the schedule, Tuesday night's vice presidential debate between J.D. Vance and Tim Walz could be the last debate of the 2024 campaign between the Republican and Democratic tickets.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Should moderators fact-check? Veteran broadcast debate host, journalism professor weigh in
item.description: MPR News host emeritus Gary Eichten and University of Minnesota associate professor and director of the Minnesota Journalism Center Benjamin Toff joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer to talk about the positives and perils of live political debates.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Unearthed photo shows Tim Walz appointee decorated house with posters of murderous communist dictators
item.description: Ida Rukavina, appointed by Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz to lead the state's Department of Iron Range Resources and Rehabilitation, posted a photo on social media in 2021 showing her walls decorated with communist imagery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris, Trump woo Gen Z voters in critical swing states
item.description: The Harris and Trump campaigns are stepping up their outreach to Generation Z voters in critical battlegrounds, targeting a voting bloc expected to play a key role in a tight White House race where any marginal advantage gained could make the difference.
item.dc:creator: Yash Roy, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tim Walz said he went to China 'dozens' of times, now his campaign says its 'closer to 15'
item.description: Vice presidential candidate Gov. Tim Walz exaggerated claims about how many times he traveled to China, telling people he spent much more time there than in actuality.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NRA targets Sen Sherrod Brown in 7-figure ad buy in Ohio: 'Vote like your life depends on it'
item.description: The National Rifle Association Political Victory Fund is ramping up its influence in swing state Ohio with a new seven-figure ad campaign aimed at Democrat Sen. Sherrod Brown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas executes Harris County man tied to five killings
item.description: Garcia White, who killed twin sisters, became the fifth Texas inmate put to death in 2024 after the U.S. Supreme Court declined to issue a stay of execution.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Kayla Guo, KPRC 2

item.title: How young Republicans and Democrats are whipping up votes in key districts
item.description: As the presidential race ramps up in Georgia, one vital voting demographic is mobilizing and hoping to impact the race: young people.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly, Erika Ryan, Kira Wakeam, and Courtney Dorning, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance’s time on the trail has often been overshadowed by self-made controversy
item.description: Republican Ohio Sen. JD Vance is prolific on the campaign trail with speeches and talking to reporters, but his messaging has at times been overshadowed by controversy often of his own making.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Walz and Vance go in depth on policy while attacking each other’s running mates in VP debate
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance are criticizing each other’s running mates in a vice presidential debate that opened with a discussion of burgeoning domestic and international troubles — a hurricane that ravaged much of the southeast U.S. and growing fears of a regional Middle East war.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris says she backs legalizing marijuana, going further than Biden
item.description: Vice President Harris reaffirmed her support for legalizing marijuana Monday, speaking up on the issue publicly for the first time she became the Democratic nominee.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Harris and Trump change their campaign plans after Hurricane Helene's destruction
item.description: Hurricane Helene is scrambling the presidential candidates' plans this week. Democratic nominee Kamala Harris is cutting short a campaign visit to Las Vegas to return to Washington for briefings. Republican candidate Donald Trump is heading to Georgia to see the storm's impact.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, JILL COLVIN and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden administration expands asylum restrictions at southern border
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it will expand its asylum restrictions at the U.S.-Mexico border, cementing tougher standards that will cut off access to protections for many migrants.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers fear 'chaos' if Trump-Harris race goes to the wire
item.description: Democratic senators say they fear Trump and his allies will seize on any initial uncertainty over the results to claim election fraud.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump turns to darker rhetoric as voting begins
item.description: In a pair of weekend events in key swing states, Trump claimed Harris is "mentally disabled" and went on to suggest police violence could curb crime.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Democrats fight to hang on to Nevada in Harris-Trump race
item.description: Democrats are fighting tooth and nail to keep Nevada from slipping away from them this cycle after narrowly winning it in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Austrian far-right party wins national vote but its chances of governing are unclear
item.description: The Freedom Party on Sunday secured the first far-right national parliamentary election victory in post-World War II Austria. But political rivals have said they won’t work with the party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Vance and Walz debate in New York City on Tuesday. Here’s what you need to know
item.description: The CBS News vice presidential debate will be the only time Ohio Sen. JD Vance and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz get to face off ahead of the 2024 election. Here's what to expect.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Jimmy Carter's 100th birthday to be celebrated with concert, new homes
item.description: Considering Carter's legacy as a philanthropist, it's no surprise that he wants any gift-giving to go to other people.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY and GLENN GAMBOA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers fear 'chaos' if Trump-Harris race goes to the wire
item.description: Lawmakers in both parties are bracing themselves for a messy aftermath to Election Day as polls show the race between former President Trump and Vice President Harris is so close in several battleground states that it could take days to determine the winner.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Tim Walz’s experience in China could help him as veep, but he barely mentions it as a candidate
item.description: When Gov. Tim Walz first ran for Congress in 2006, his campaign proudly touted his extensive experience in China. In fact, earlier in his political career, he occasionally exaggerated it. But now that he’s running for vice president, Walz barely mentions China.
item.dc:creator: Forest Hunt, Caspar von Au, and Curtis Gilbert, MPR News

item.title: Exploring the Eric Adams indictment for bribery, wire fraud, and solicitating contributions from Turkish nationals
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams pleaded not guilty in a federal bribery case after being indicted on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PIX Panel discusses historic indictment against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – History was made in New York City this week when Eric Adams became the city’s first sitting mayor to be indicted on federal charges. A federal indictment charged Adams with bribery, campaign finance and conspiracy offenses.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11
